slides.title: Late Static Binding
Magic Methods
Magic Method Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
class Stringable {
    private $value;
    public function __construct($value) {
        $this->value = $value;
    }
    public function __toString() {
        return (string) $this->value;
    }
}
$obj = new Stringable(123);
echo $obj;
?>

quizData.question: What is the difference between self:: and static::?

options.text: self:: refers to the current class, static:: to the called class at runtime
static:: is for static methods only
self:: is for static methods only

quizData.explanation: self:: uses the class where it's written; static:: uses the class that was actually called (late static binding).
